questions.q: Define matter and its fundamental properties.
questions.topic: Chapter 1: Some Basic Concepts of Chemistry
questions.answer: Matter is anything that has mass and occupies space. The fundamental properties of matter are mass, volume, and density.

questions.q: What are the differences between pure and impure substances?
questions.topic: Chapter 1: Some Basic Concepts of Chemistry
questions.answer: A pure substance has a fixed composition and properties, whereas an impure substance has a variable composition and properties. Pure substances are homogeneous, whereas impure substances are heterogeneous.

questions.q: Explain the law of conservation of mass.
questions.topic: Chapter 1: Some Basic Concepts of Chemistry
questions.answer: The law of conservation of mass states that mass cannot be created or destroyed in a chemical reaction. The total mass of the reactants is equal to the total mass of the products.

questions.q: Distinguish between physical and chemical changes.
questions.topic: Chapter 1: Some Basic Concepts of Chemistry
questions.answer: Physical changes involve a change in the state of a substance without changing its chemical composition, whereas chemical changes involve a change in the chemical composition of a substance. Physical changes are reversible, whereas chemical changes are irreversible.

questions.q: What is the difference between a physical property and a chemical property?
questions.topic: Chapter 1: Some Basic Concepts of Chemistry
questions.answer: Physical properties are characteristics that can be observed or measured without changing the composition of a substance, whereas chemical properties are characteristics that can only be observed or measured by changing the composition of a substance.

questions.topic: Chapter 1: Some Basic Concepts of Chemistry
questions.answer: Dalton's atomic theory states that elements are composed of small indivisible particles called atoms, atoms of the same element are identical in mass, size, and properties, atoms of different elements differ in mass, size, and properties, and atoms combine in whole number ratios to form compounds.

questions.q: Explain the concept of a molecule.
questions.topic: Chapter 1: Some Basic Concepts of Chemistry
questions.answer: A molecule is a group of two or more atoms that are chemically bonded together. Molecules can be composed of atoms of the same element or different elements.

questions.q: What is the difference between a compound and a molecule?
questions.topic: Chapter 1: Some Basic Concepts of Chemistry
questions.answer: A compound is a substance formed by the chemical combination of two or more elements, whereas a molecule is a group of atoms that are chemically bonded together. A compound can be composed of one or more molecules.

questions.q: Explain the concept of the mole.
questions.answer: The mole is a unit of measurement that represents 6.022 x 10^23 particles (atoms, molecules, or ions). The mole is used to express the amount of a substance and is related to the Avogadro's constant.

questions.q: State the importance of the periodic table.
questions.answer: The periodic table is a tabular arrangement of elements that shows their properties and relationships. It is useful for predicting the properties of elements and the products of chemical reactions.
